questions.question: The respiratory system allows oxygen to enter the blood. The oxygen is necessary for cellular production of _______.
Pulmonary ventilation is also known as:
Exchange of gases between the air and blood is _____ and the exchange of gases between blood and tissue fluid is ______.
Identify the function of the mucous membrane lining respiratory airways.
options: exchange gases and warm and moisten air.
exchange gases and clean and warm air.
exchange gases and moisten and warm air.

questions.question: Identify the structures that aid in the cleansing of the air.
options: blood vessels, cilia and coarse nasal hairs
blood vessels coarse nasal hairs and mucus
cilia, coarse nasal hairs and mucous

questions.question: The function of the nasal conchae is to increase surface area:
options: for cleansing, moisturizing, and warming the air.

questions.question: Identify structures of the upper respiratory tract.
options: Alveoli, nasal cavity, pharynx and trachea.
Bronchus, nasal cavity, pharynx and larynx.
Nasal cavity, pharynx, glottis and larynx.
Trachea, nasal cavity pharynx and larynx.

questions.question: Identify the sections of the pharynx in order as air is inhaled.

The C-shaped cartilaginous rings around the trachea are open posteriorly to:
options: allow for expansion of the esophagus during swallowing
allow the vocal cords to relax
prevent food from entering the nasal cavity during swallowing
prevent food from entering the trachea

nose, pharynx, larynx, bronchi, trachea, lungs
nose, pharynx, larynx, trachea, bronchi, lungs

options: inspiratory reserve volume + expiratory reserve volume.

options: elevated carbon dioxide and hydrogen ions

questions.question: The exchange of respiratory gases in the lungs is called
The driving factor(s) for gas exchange is (are)
options: different partial pressures of the blood gases.
the number of white blood cells in the blood.
different concentrations of the blood gases.

questions.question: External respiration occurs between the alveoli and the pulmonary capillaries because the concentration of oxygen levels is higher in the ______ and the concentration of carbon dioxide is higher in the ______.
The exchange of gases between the blood and tissue fluids is called

Most of the carbon dioxide in the blood is transported as
options: carbon dioxide dissolved in the plasma.

questions.question: Carbonic acid can be formed from
options: A and B, but not C.

questions.question: Most of the oxygen in the blood is transported
options: attached to the heme group of the hemoglobin in the red blood cells.
attached to the globin part of the hemoglobin in the red blood cells.
as bicarbonate ions in the red blood cells.

questions.question: What ion must enter a red blood cell so that bicarbonate ions can move into the plasma for transport?

options: a gas dissolved in the plasma
as bicarbonate ion in the plasma
attached to the heme group of hemoglobin

options: It is a restrictive pulmonary disorder.

questions.question: Describe the correct sequence of changes leading to lung cancer.
options: cilia lost, thickening and callusing of cells, disordered cells, atypical nuclei, metastasis
Thickening and callusing of cells, cilia lost, atypical nuclei, disordered cells, metastasis
atypical nuclei, disordered cells, metastasis, thickening and callusing of cells, cilia lost
disordered cells, metastasis, cilia lost, thickening and callusing of cells, atypical nuclei
